title: Successful Login with Username (Non-Email) Routes to Correct Role-Based Dashboard
description: Verifies that the system correctly accepts a plain username (not email format) as a valid credential identifier, authenticates the user, and redirects to the role-appropriate landing page — confirming the authentication pipeline handles both identifier types and that role resolution is correct post-login.
expected_result: PASS: Login with a plain username succeeds, user lands on /supervisor/dashboard, role badge reads 'Supervisor', and user identity matches 'ops.supervisor_42'. FAIL: System rejects the username as invalid (treats it as malformed email), redirects to the wrong dashboard, assigns the wrong role, or displays another user's identity.

test_steps.action: Fill in the username field with a plain username (no @ symbol, no domain)

test_steps.expected: User is authenticated and redirected to the supervisor-specific dashboard URL

test_steps.action: Verify the role indicator in the UI reflects 'Supervisor' — not 'Admin' or 'User'
test_steps.expected: Role badge element is visible and displays 'Supervisor'

test_steps.action: Confirm the username is displayed correctly in the user profile area
test_steps.expected: User menu displays 'ops.supervisor_42' as the logged-in identity
